BERK KOCAMAN (EUW) |checkboxAutoTeams=Yes |checkbox2=No |role=Coach |issub1=No |istrainee1=No |issub2=No |istrainee2=No |issub3=No |istrainee3=No |issub4=No |istrainee4=No |issub5=No |istrainee5=No |checkbox3=No |stream=https://bj.afreecatv.com/wlswlfkffk |facebook=https://www.facebook.com/profile.php?id=100013261069353 |twitter=youthcrewcoco |youtube=https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCvrFt6I9_6OAi0Id7meT7XQ |deeplol=https://www.deeplol.gg/pro/Coco |checkboxSuppressOrgNavbox=No |checkboxSBS=Yes |checkboxTOCR=Yes |text=His name was previously stylized '''CoCo'''. |checkboxIsSub=No |checkbox-res=No |checkboxPrev=Yes |prev1=Mid }} ==Biography== ===Season 3=== CoCo was picked up by the [[Xenics]] organization in January 2013 and was immediately made the starting mid laner. His first OGN was [[HOT6iX Champions Summer 2013]] where they failed to make it past the group stage. ===2014 Season=== CoCo continued to play for Xenics in [[PANDORA.TV Champions Winter 2013-2014]] where they made it to the quarterfinals but lost to [[NaJin White Shield]] 3-1. Soon after, CoCo left Xenics and joined [[CJ Entus Frost]]. Frost fell in the quarters again at [[HOT6iX Champions Spring 2014]], losing 3-1 to [[Samsung Blue]]. [[HOT6iX Champions Summer 2014]] didn't go much better for Frost and they failed to make it out of groups which caused them to miss the [[2014 Season Korea Regional Finals]]. ===2015 Season=== Frost and their sister team [[CJ Entus Blaze|Blaze]] merged after the changes to the OGN rules. Blaze's mid laner, [[Ambition]], was moved to the jungle and CoCo was made the starting mid laner. They were invited to [[IEM Season IX - World Championship|IEM Katowice]] in March 2015. They finished in 5th/6th place, after defeating [[Gambit Gaming]] but then losing to [[Team SoloMid]] and [[Team WE]]. CJ participated in [[SBENU Champions Spring 2015]] where they lost in the semifinals 3-2 to [[SK Telecom T1]]. CJ placed third in the regular season of [[SBENU Champions Summer 2015]] but lost 3-0 to the [[KOO Tigers]] in the second round of the playoffs. CJ had enough circuit points to qualify for the [[2015 Season Korea Regional Finals]] but they lost 3-2 to the [[Jin Air Green Wings]]. ===2016 Preseason=== CJ played in the [[2015 LoL KeSPA Cup]] where they lost 3-0 in the finals to [[ESC Ever|Ever]]. On the 30th of November, CoCo left CJ Entus. He joined [[Incredible Miracle]] on December 5. Incredible Miracle rebranded to [[Longzhu]] on January 3, 2016. ===2016 Season=== During the [[LCK/2016 Season/Spring Season|2016 LCK Spring Season]], Longzhu played with a 10-man roster, liberally using substitute players. Coco shared play time with fellow mid laner [[Frozen (Kim Tae-il)|Frozen]]. Longzhu finished the spring split in seventh place with an 8-10 set record, missing out on a spot in the playoffs. Longzhu fared poorly in the [[LCK/2016 Season/Summer Season|2016 LCK Summer Season]], going 7-11 in sets and netting them eighth place, letting them escape relegations. == Trivia == * Had a game in which he went 21/0/8 as Kassadin in [[OGN Club Masters]] * On February 7, 2015, set the record for highest CS in a competitive match - 726 on {{ci|Jayce}} in a 79:48 minute loss to [[Jin Air]] during [[SBENU Champions Spring 2015|Champions Spring 2015]]. The previous record holder was [[ROBERTxLEE]] with 719 CS on {{ci|Lucian}}. This record was held for over a year before being broken by [[Froggen]] on March 3, 2016, in a game with 764 CS on {{ci|Gangplank}}. {{PlayerStatsTrivia}} ==Tournament Results== {{PlayerResults|show=overviewpage}} {{PlayerShowmatchResults|show=overviewpage}} {{PlayerResults1v1|show=overviewpage}} == Media == {{PlayerMedia}} ===Interviews=== {{TDRight |name1=2015 |name2=2016}} {{TDRight|tab}} * January 15, [http://barontimer.tumblr.com/post/137368304436/with-this-victory-trust-was-established “With this victory, trust was established” - Interview with Longzhu’s CoCo and Expession] ''Translated by Baron Timer'' {{TDRight|tab}} * January 22, [http://www.liquidlegends.net/forum/lol-general/476179-interview-lck-w3d1-cj-vs-kt-coco CJ Coco Interview] ''with Inven (Translated by TL)'' * November 7, [http://barontimer.tumblr.com/post/132759210776/well-show-you-much-better-form-in-the “We’ll show you much better form in the Quarterfinals” - Interview with CJ Entus’ Coco] ''translated by Grace (xoprestige)'' ([http://www.inven.co.kr/webzine/news/?news=146107&site=lol Original Interview (Korean)]) {{TDRight/end}} ===Highlights=== {{TDRight |name1=2015}} {{TDRight|tab}} * February 5 - [http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=A4ti2BRHhFY Azubu Minis S1:14 - League of Legends - CJ Entus Coco] ''by Azubu'' {{TDRight|end}} === Images === {{PlayerProfileGallery}} {{PlayerPageEnd}}
